organism: Bordetella pertussis (strain Tohama I / ATCC BAA-589 / NCTC 13251)

machinery_genes.role: inner-membrane ABC transporter / ATPase that energises export and forms the IM channel
machinery_genes.quote: The gene products of cyaB and cyaD are highly similar to HlyB and HlyD, known to be necessary for the transport of HlyA across the cell envelope and for its release into the external medium.
machinery_genes.note: Glaser et al 1988 founding paper. cyaB lies immediately downstream of the cyaA toxin gene. CyaB is the HlyB homologue = the T1SS ABC transporter; HlyB role (IM ATPase channel) from apparatus brief vocabulary. Supporting quote in Masure et al 1990 (PMID 2182114): 'CyaB, CyaD, and CyaE are required for the secretion of CyaA from Bordetella pertussis.'

machinery_genes.role: periplasm-spanning IM-anchored adaptor (MFP) bridging the ABC transporter to the OMP
machinery_genes.quote: The gene products of cyaB and cyaD are highly similar to HlyB and HlyD, known to be necessary for the transport of HlyA across the cell envelope and for its release into the external medium.
machinery_genes.note: Glaser et al 1988. CyaD is the HlyD homologue = the T1SS membrane fusion protein (adaptor); HlyD/MFP role from apparatus brief vocabulary. Required for secretion per Masure et al 1990 (PMID 2182114).

machinery_genes.role: TolC-family outer-membrane channel forming the exit duct
machinery_genes.quote: the B.pertussis adenylate cyclase-haemolysin bifunctional protein is secreted by a mechanism similar to that described for E.coli alpha-haemolysin, requiring, in addition to the cyaB and cyaD gene products, the presence of a third gene product specified by the cyaE gene
machinery_genes.note: Glaser et al 1988 name cyaE as the third gene product required for secretion (the E. coli HlyA system uses TolC as its OMP; CyaE is the dedicated OMP of the cya T1SS, the third apparatus component). OMP/TolC-family role assigned from apparatus brief vocabulary. Masure et al 1990 (PMID 2182114) confirms: 'CyaB, CyaD, and CyaE are required for the secretion of CyaA from Bordetella pertussis.' This is the supporting (apparatus) paper.

notes: System: the cya operon T1SS of Bordetella pertussis, which secretes the bifunctional calmodulin-sensitive adenylate cyclase-haemolysin toxin CyaA (cyclolysin, an RTX toxin; the curated effector BP0760). The three-component T1SS apparatus is encoded immediately downstream of cyaA in the operon cyaA-cyaB-cyaD-cyaE: CyaB (ABC transporter, HlyB-like), CyaD (membrane fusion protein, HlyD-like), CyaE (outer-membrane TolC-family channel). The founding paper (Glaser et al 1988, EMBO J) identified cyaB, cyaD and cyaE as the genes required for CyaA export and showed CyaB/CyaD are direct homologues of the E. coli haemolysin transport proteins HlyB/HlyD; Masure et al 1990 (Biochemistry) independently confirmed all three (CyaB, CyaD, CyaE) are required for CyaA secretion. Apparatus papers used the Tohama-derived B. pertussis cya locus, matching this instance directly (no cross-strain transfer needed). Core T1SS apparatus fully covered (3/3 components). Deliberately excluded: the secreted substrate CyaA/cyclolysin (the effector, not machinery), and any maturation/regulatory functions. Note CyaA itself is the substrate (its acylation is by CyaC, an acyltransferase encoded separately, which is a maturation enzyme not transport apparatus and was not in scope here). The OM/MFP/ABC molecular role labels for CyaB/CyaD/CyaE follow the apparatus brief's T1SS vocabulary, anchored to the verbatim naming quotes from Glaser 1988.
